title: Cat Double Canine Teeth: Understanding Persistent Deciduous Teeth
meta_desc: Noticed your kitten has two sets of fangs? Learn about persistent baby teeth, why it's a low-urgency issue, and how a photo helps your veterinarian.
content_html:
What Are Double Canine Teeth?

If you see two fangs on one side of your cat's mouth, you are likely looking at persistent deciduous teeth. This happens when the adult canine tooth erupts from the gum line before the baby tooth has fallen out. Instead of the baby tooth being pushed out, the two teeth sit side-by-side, which is common in kittens between 4 and 7 months old.

Is This a Veterinary Emergency?

The urgency level for this condition is Low. While it is not an immediate medical emergency, it does require a scheduled veterinary visit. If left untreated, the extra tooth can cause overcrowding, trap food debris, lead to early-onset periodontal disease, or cause the adult tooth to grow in at an incorrect angle, potentially causing pain or injury to the roof of the mouth.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Taking a clear photo of your cat's teeth allows a veterinary professional to see the exact positioning of the redundant teeth. A photo helps us determine if there is active gum inflammation or if the adult tooth is being displaced. This visual information helps us decide whether the extraction can wait until your cat's spay or neuter surgery or if it needs to be addressed sooner to prevent dental complications.

faqs.question: At what age should a kitten's baby teeth be gone?
faqs.answer: Most kittens lose all their deciduous (baby) teeth by 6 to 7 months of age.

faqs.question: Will the baby tooth eventually fall out on its own?
faqs.answer: In some cases it might, but if the adult tooth is already fully visible and the baby tooth is still firm, it likely needs to be professionally extracted.

faqs.question: Can I pull the loose baby tooth myself?
faqs.answer: No, you should never attempt to pull a cat's tooth. The roots are very long and fragile; breaking the root can lead to infection and significant pain.
